name: observability
description: kube-prometheus-stack, Grafana, Loki, Thanos, Tempo, dashboards, AlertManager.
sprintGoal: Deploy the full observability stack — Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Thanos, and Tempo — with Ceph-backed storage, Keycloak SSO, and ArgoCD GitOps integration.

stories.title: Helm chart: kube-prometheus-stack
stories.description: Create charts/prometheus-stack/ wrapping prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack. Grafana at grafana.{{ .Values.global.domain }} with Keycloak OIDC. Prometheus retention: 15 days (Thanos handles long-term). AlertManager with a stub for Slack/email routing. Pre-load dashboards: Node Exporter, Kubernetes cluster, GitLab, Ceph (from rook-ceph chart), Istio. Persistent storage on ceph-block.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard ConfigMaps for Node, K8s, GitLab, Ceph, Istio
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Specific score 3: no pinned upstream chart version for prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack; Keycloak OIDC clientId/realm/secret reference path unspecified; 5 dashboard sources named but no Grafana dashboard IDs provided (1860, 315, etc.) leaving fetch scope open-ended. Measurable score 3: 'Keycloak OIDC auth for Grafana' is not a binary shell command — must be `helm template | grep genericOAuth` or equivalent; 'AlertManager stub config included' needs `helm template | grep -A5 receivers`; 'charts/prometheus-stack/ with Grafana on global.domain' is a structural description, not a verifiable command. Achievable score 3: sourcing 5 external dashboard JSONs plus OIDC wiring at only 2 story points is at the budget ceiling — dashboard IDs (e.g. grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/1860) would remove open-ended sourcing work. TimeBound score 4: dashboard sourcing without pinned IDs could expand scope; adding IDs (e.g. 1860, 315, 7249, 2842, 7645) would bring this to 5.

stories.title: Helm chart: Loki log aggregation
stories.description: Create charts/loki/ wrapping grafana/loki (pin appVersion to a specific release) in Simple Scalable mode with ceph-bucket storage. Include a Grafana datasource ConfigMap in charts/loki/templates/ so Loki auto-registers in Grafana. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/observability/loki-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/loki/Chart.yaml pins appVersion to a specific Loki release (e.g. 3.0.0)
charts/loki/values.yaml sets storage.type to s3 with ceph-bucket endpoint
helm lint charts/loki/ exits 0 with 0 chart(s) failed
argocd-apps/observability/loki-app.yaml exists and is valid YAML
stories.testPlan: helm lint charts/loki/; helm template charts/loki/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template charts/loki/ | grep datasource; cat argocd-apps/observability/loki-app.yaml | yq e '.' -

smart.notes: Specific score 4: appVersion is given only as an example ('e.g. 3.0.0') rather than a required pinned version — Ralph must choose a specific release and this should be prescribed to avoid divergence. All other details (Simple Scalable mode, ceph-bucket, datasource ConfigMap location, ArgoCD app path) are fully specified. All other SMART dimensions green.

stories.title: Helm chart: Thanos long-term Prometheus storage
stories.description: Create charts/thanos/ wrapping bitnami/thanos (pin appVersion). Configure sidecar mode connecting to Prometheus with ceph-bucket as the object store backend. Include a Grafana datasource ConfigMap in charts/thanos/templates/ so the Thanos query endpoint auto-registers in Grafana. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/observability/thanos-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/thanos/Chart.yaml pins appVersion to a specific Thanos release
charts/thanos/values.yaml configures objstoreConfig pointing to ceph-bucket
helm lint charts/thanos/ exits 0 with 0 chart(s) failed
argocd-apps/observability/thanos-app.yaml exists and is valid YAML
stories.testPlan: helm lint charts/thanos/; helm template charts/thanos/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template charts/thanos/ | grep sidecar; cat argocd-apps/observability/thanos-app.yaml | yq e '.' -

smart.notes: Specific score 4: upstream bitnami/thanos version not pinned (only 'pin appVersion' is instructed without a value); sidecar mode requires the Prometheus service endpoint/namespace but this is left for Ralph to infer — should specify `prometheus-stack.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:9090` or equivalent. Achievable score 4: sidecar cross-chart wiring adds slight complexity but is well-understood; 2 points is tight but viable given the prescriptive ACs. All other SMART dimensions green.

stories.title: Helm chart: Tempo distributed tracing
stories.description: Create charts/tempo/ wrapping grafana/tempo-distributed (pin appVersion). Configure ceph-bucket as the storage backend. Include a Grafana datasource ConfigMap in charts/tempo/templates/ so Tempo auto-registers in Grafana. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/observability/tempo-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/tempo/Chart.yaml pins appVersion to a specific Tempo release
charts/tempo/values.yaml sets storage backend to s3 with ceph-bucket endpoint
helm lint charts/tempo/ exits 0 with 0 chart(s) failed
argocd-apps/observability/tempo-app.yaml exists and is valid YAML
stories.testPlan: helm lint charts/tempo/; helm template charts/tempo/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template charts/tempo/ | grep datasource; cat argocd-apps/observability/tempo-app.yaml | yq e '.' -

smart.notes: Specific score 4: grafana/tempo-distributed version not pinned (only 'pin appVersion' instructed without a value); ceph-bucket endpoint/bucket name not specified — Ralph must infer from prior stories. All ACs are precise binary commands and scope is tightly bounded. Achievable score 4: 2-point budget is tight for wrapping a distributed-mode chart but achievable since the pattern mirrors 026a and 026b. All other SMART dimensions green.
